---
title: "Section 4: Statisical Analysis"
author: "My Ha"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

## Statistical Analysis

Section: 02

Name: My Ha

## References

To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>

### Install Packages- comment out when complete

```{r}
#install.packages(c("rlang", "vctrs", "pillar", "tibble", "dplyr"))
```

## Install Libraries

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
```

## Set Working Directory and load file into that location

```{r}
getwd()
```

## Load Data Dictionary Image and Data from your Working Directory

```{r}
Section4Data <-read_xlsx("Section_4_Exercise.xlsx", sheet = "Exercise")
glimpse(Section4Data)
```

```{r}
# Use the Global environment to view Section3Data
```

## Data Dictionary

![Section 4 Exercise Datatypes](Data%20Dictionary%20Image.png){fig-align="center"}

## Statistical Analysis

### Offer Price - Bid Price from buyer

####Statistical Summary

```{r}
Offer_Price_Summary <- summary(Section4Data$Offer_Price)
print(Offer_Price_Summary)
```

### Natural Logarithm for Offer Price

```{r}
Section4Data$Offer_Price_LN <- log(Section4Data$Offer_Price)
```

### ZScore Scale for OfferPRice

```{r}
Section4Data$Offer_Price_ZScale<- scale(Section4Data$Offer_Price)
```

### Histogram

```{r}
Offer_Price_Histogram<-ggplot(Section4Data, aes(x = Offer_Price)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
labs(title = "Histogram of Offer Price",
x = "Offer Price",
y = "Count") +
theme_minimal()
print(Offer_Price_Histogram)
```

### Histogram of Offer_Price Scaling

```{r}
Offer_Price_LN_Histogram<-ggplot(Section4Data, aes(x = Offer_Price_LN)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
labs(title = "Histogram of LN(Offer Price)",
x = "LN(Offer Price)",
y = "Count") +
theme_minimal()
print(Offer_Price_LN_Histogram)
```

### Histogram of Offer_Price ZScale

```{r}
Offer_Price_ZScale_Histogram<-ggplot(Section4Data,
aes(x = Offer_Price_ZScale)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
labs(title = "Histogram of ZScale(Offer Price)",
x = "ZScale(Offer Price)",
y = "Count") +
theme_minimal()
print(Offer_Price_ZScale_Histogram)
```

### Sales Price - Bid Price from buyer

####Statistical Summary

```{r}
Sales_Price_Summary <- summary(Section4Data$Sales_Price)
print(Sales_Price_Summary)
```

### Natural Logarithm for Sales Price

```{r}
Section4Data$Sales_Price_LN <- log(Section4Data$Sales_Price)
```

### Natural Logarithm for Sales Price

```{r}
Section4Data$Sales_Price_LN <- log(Section4Data$Sales_Price)
```

### ZScore Scale for Sales Price

```{r}
Section4Data$Sales_Price_ZScale<- scale(Section4Data$Sales_Price)
```

### Histogram

```{r}
Sales_Price_Histogram<-ggplot(Section4Data, aes(x = Sales_Price)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
labs(title = "Histogram of Offer Price",
x = "Offer Price",
y = "Count") +
theme_minimal()
print(Sales_Price_Histogram)
```

### Histogram of Sales_Price Scaling

```{r}
Sales_Price_LN_Histogram<-ggplot(Section4Data, aes(x = Sales_Price_LN)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
labs(title = "Histogram of LN(Offer Price)",
x = "LN(Offer Price)",
y = "Count") +
theme_minimal()
print(Sales_Price_LN_Histogram)
```

### Histogram of Sales_Price ZScale

```{r}
Sales_Price_ZScale_Histogram<-ggplot(Section4Data, aes(x = Sales_Price_ZScale)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
labs(title = "Histogram of ZScale(Offer Price)",
x = "ZScale(Offer Price)",
y = "Count") +
theme_minimal()
print(Sales_Price_ZScale_Histogram)
```

### Delta Price - Bid Price from buyer

####Statistical Summary

```{r}
Delta_Summary <- summary(Section4Data$Delta)
print(Delta_Summary)
```

### Natural Logarithm for Delta

```{r}
Section4Data$Delta_LN <- log(Section4Data$Delta)
```

### ZScore Scale for Delta

```{r}
Section4Data$Delta_ZScale<- scale(Section4Data$Delta)
```

### Histogram

```{r}
Delta_Histogram<-ggplot(Section4Data, aes(x = Delta)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
labs(title = "Histogram of Delta",
x = "Offer Price",
y = "Count") +
theme_minimal()
print(Delta_Histogram)
```

### Histogram of Delta Scaling

```{r}
Delta_LN_Histogram<-ggplot(Section4Data, aes(x = Delta_LN)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
labs(title = "Histogram of LN(Delta)",
x = "LN(Offer Price)",
y = "Count") +
theme_minimal()
print(Delta_LN_Histogram)
```

### Histogram of Delta ZScale

```{r}
Delta_ZScale_Histogram<-ggplot(Section4Data, aes(x = Delta_ZScale)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
labs(title = "Histogram of ZScale(Offer Price)",
x = "ZScale(Delta)",
y = "Count") +
theme_minimal()
print(Delta_ZScale_Histogram)
```

## Products

### Histogram of Products (Categorical Variable)

```{r}
Product_Histogram <- ggplot(Section4Data, aes(x = Products)) +
geom_bar(fill = "#1b9e77", color = "black", width = 0.7) +
labs(title = "Histogram of Products",
x = "Product Type",
y = "Count") +
theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
print(Product_Histogram)
```

### Subsetting by Products

```{r}
hats <- Section4Data %>% filter(Products == "hat")
sunglasses <- Section4Data %>% filter(Products == "sunglasses")
jackets <- Section4Data %>% filter(Products == "jacket")
```

## Sales

### Histogram of Sale (Categorical Variable)

```{r}
Sale_Histogram <- ggplot(Section4Data, aes(x = Sale)) +
geom_bar(fill = "#1b9e77", color = "black", width = 0.7) +
labs(title = "Histogram of Sale",
x = "Sale",
y = "Count") +
theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
print(Sale_Histogram)
```

## Bivariate Analyses

### Chi Square Test of Independence for Products and Sale

### Interpretation guide:

If p \< 0.05, there is evidence of an association between Product type and Sale outcome. If p \> 0.05, there is no evidence of a relationship — meaning sale rates do not differ by product type.

```{r}
tab <- table(Section4Data$Products, Section4Data$Sale)
tab
```

```{r}
# 3. Run Chi-Squared Test of Independence
chisq.test(tab)
```

### Logistic Regression for Delta and sale

```{r}
# Convert Sale to a binary factor (1 = yes, 0 = no)
df <- Section4Data %>%
mutate(Sale_bin = ifelse(Sale == "yes", 1, 0))
# Fit logistic regression model
model <- glm(Sale_bin ~ Delta, data = df, family = binomial)
```

```{r}
# View model summary
summary(model)
```

```{r}
# Create predicted probabilities across Delta range
Section4Data <- Section4Data %>%
mutate(Sale_bin = ifelse(Sale == "yes", 1, 0))
newdata <- data.frame(Delta = seq(min(Section4Data$Delta),
max(Section4Data$Delta), length.out = 200))
newdata$pred <- predict(model, newdata, type = "response")
# Plot
Logistic_Regression_Sale_Delta <- ggplot(Section4Data,
aes(x = Delta, y = Sale_bin)) +
geom_jitter(height = 0.05, width = 0.1, alpha = 0.4, color = "steelblue") +
geom_line(data = newdata, aes(x = Delta, y = pred),
color = "darkred", linewidth = 1.2) +
labs(title = "Logistic Regression: Probability of Sale vs Delta",
x = "Delta",
y = "Probability of Sale") +
theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
print(Logistic_Regression_Sale_Delta)
```

## Correlations for Continuous Variables

```{r}
Section4Data %>%
select(Offer_Price, Sales_Price, Delta) %>%
cor(use = "complete.obs")
```

## Covariance Actual Values

```{r}
cov(Section4Data[, c("Offer_Price", "Sales_Price", "Delta")],
use = "complete.obs")
```

```{r}
cov_mat <- cov(Section4Data[, c("Offer_Price", "Sales_Price", "Delta")])
cov_df <- melt(cov_mat)
ggplot(cov_df, aes(Var1, Var2, fill = value)) + geom_tile() +
scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#3498DB", high = "#E74C3C",
mid = "white", midpoint = 0) +
theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
labs(title = "Covariance Matrix Heatmap", fill = "Covariance")
```

## Regression of Offer_Price and Sales_Price

```{r}
Offer_v_Sales <- ggplot(Section4Data, aes(x = Offer_Price, y = Sales_Price)) +
geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
labs(
title = "Offer_v_Sales",
x = "Offer",
y = "Sales"
) +
theme_minimal()
print(Offer_v_Sales)
```

```{r}
model <- lm(Sales_Price ~ Offer_Price, data = Section4Data)
summary(model)
```

```{r}
print(model)
```

## BoxPlots

```{r}
# Select the three numeric variables
num_df <- Section4Data %>%
select(Offer_Price, Sales_Price)
# Convert to long format
long_df <- num_df %>%
pivot_longer(cols = everything(),
names_to = "Variable",
values_to = "Value")
# Boxplot of all variables in one figure
ggplot(long_df, aes(x = Variable, y = Value, fill = Variable)) +
geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
labs(title = "Boxplot of Offer Price, and Sales Price",
x = "Variable",
y = "Value") +
theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
theme(legend.position = "none")
```

```{r}
# Select the three numeric variables
num_df <- Section4Data %>%
select(Delta)
# Convert to long format
long_df <- num_df %>%
pivot_longer(cols = everything(),
names_to = "Variable",
values_to = "Value")
# Boxplot of all variables in one figure
ggplot(long_df, aes(x = Variable, y = Value, fill = Variable)) +
geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
labs(title = "Boxplot of Delta",
x = "Variable",
y = "Value") +
theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
theme(legend.position = "none")
```
